session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant:
Now I understand the skill requirements. Let me execute it step by step:
1. First, fetch PR feedback using the erk exec command 2. Get file restructuring context using git diff 3. Classify the comments according to the model 4. Output structured JSON

**Branch:** `plnd/O9009-add-agent-friendly-c-03-08-1017`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9014#issuecomment-4019133447" }, { "id": 4019148861, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-08 07:30:50 PT

Found 3 violations across 1 file. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
❌ Verbatim code blocks — Found in docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md:247, :274
❌ Drift risk (local path) — Found in docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md:308
✅ Inaccurate claims — None found

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md:247`: Verbatim copy of `exit_with_error` from `src/erk/cli/script_output.py:44-49`
- `docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md:274`: Verbatim copy of `OneShotDispatchResult` from `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py:47-53`
- `docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md:308`: Drift risk — user-local path `/Users/schrockn/code/githubs/googleworkspace/cli` inaccessible to agents

### Files Reviewed
- `docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md`: 3 violations
- `docs/learned/cli/index.md`: 0 violations (index update only)
- `docs/learned/cli/tripwires.md`: 0 violations (tripwire entries only)
- `docs/learned/tripwires-index.md`: 0 violations (count update only)

### Doc Verdict
| File | Verdict | Duplicative % | High Value % | Issues |
|------|---------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| `docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md` | SIMPLIFY | 15% | 65% | 2 verbatim, 1 drift risk |

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-08 07:30:50 PT**: Found 3 violations in new doc agent-friendly-cli.md: 2 verbatim code blocks (exit_with_error, OneShotDispatchResult), 1 drift risk (user-local path to gws repo)

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me analyze and classify the feedback:
**Analysis:**
From the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` output for PR 9014:
**PR Details:** - PR Number: 9014 - PR Title: "Add agent-friendly CLI design principles documentation" - PR URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9014
**Comments:** 1. **ID 4019131221** (author: schrockn) - Plan Queued for Implementation - This is a machine-generated metadata comment, informational only 2. **ID 4019132001** (author: github-actions[bot]) - GitHub Action Started - CI status update, informational 3. **ID 4019133447** (author: github-actions[bot]) - Starting implementation - CI status update, informational 4. **ID 4019148861** (author: github-actions[bot]) - Audit PR Docs - **ACTIONABLE** - This is a bot audit finding 3 violations: - 2 verbatim code blocks (drift/duplication risk) - 1 drift risk (user-local path)
**File Restructuring Context:** The PR adds new files (agent-friendly-cli.md and updates to index/tripwires) with no file moves or renames detected.
**Classification:**
The audit comment (4019148861) is actionable because it identifies specific code quality issues in the new documentation: - Verbatim copies that should be referenced instead - Local path that creates maintenance risk
**Complexity:** single_file (all violations are in docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md) **Pre-existing:** false (no bot comment on moved code; issue is specific to new content) **Classification:** actionable (requires documentation fixes)
Now I'll output the complete JSON:
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9014, "pr_title": "Add agent-friendly CLI design principles documentation", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9014", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "discussion_4019148861", "type": "discussion", "path": "docs/learned/cli/agent-friendly-cli.md", "line": null, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Fix 3 audit violations: remove 2 verbatim code blocks and replace user-local path reference with accessible path", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "Found 3 violations: 2 verbatim copies (exit_with_error, OneShotDispatchResult) and 1 drift risk (inaccessible user-local path /Users/schrockn/code/githubs/googleworkspace/cli)" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 3, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
